# llms.txt

# Auto-generated by coding agent on 2026-04-09
# Describes how large language models may use content from this domain.

Site: https://hive.chia.cam.ac.uk
Site-Description: Website of the HIVE CAI Lab, a research lab at the University of Cambridge studying human interactions, values, and experiences with conversational AI.
Owner: HIVE CAI Lab, Centre for Human Inspired AI (CHIA), University of Cambridge
Contact: hive@chia.cam.ac.uk
Contact-URL: https://www.chia.cam.ac.uk/
Language: en

Project-Name: HIVE CAI Lab
Project-Description: The HIVE CAI Lab (Human Interactions, Values & Experiences with Conversational AI) advances human-centred, value-sensitive conversational AI through interdisciplinary HCI and design research. The lab explores industry practices, design democratisation, and participatory frameworks and tools. It is part of the Centre for Human Inspired AI (CHIA) at the University of Cambridge and is directed by Dr. Malak Sadek.
Project-Category: academic-research-lab
Project-Launch-Date: 2026

# ---
# 1. CRAWLING & CONTENT SCOPE
# ---

LLM-Crawling: allow

Allow-Paths:
  - /                       # homepage (single-page site with all public content)
  - /sitemap.xml            # dynamically generated sitemap
  - /robots.txt             # dynamically generated robots.txt
  - /img/                   # public images directory (logos, team photos, open-graph assets)

Disallow-Paths:
  - /_next/                 # Next.js internal build artifacts and runtime files
  - /api/                   # reserved; no API routes exist currently but disallowed by default

Sitemaps:
  - /sitemap.xml

# Note: This is a single-page public website. There are no authenticated areas,
# no user accounts, no dashboards, no admin panels, no checkout flows, no API
# endpoints, and no user-generated content. All content on the site is public.

# ---
# 2. USAGE RIGHTS
# ---

Training-Usage: disallow
RAG-Usage: disallow
Commercial-Usage: disallow

Attribution-Required: yes
Attribution-Format: "Source: HIVE CAI Lab, University of Cambridge"

# ---
# 3. SENSITIVE DATA
# ---

Sensitive-Content:
  - none

# This website does not collect, store, or expose personal data, customer data,
# user accounts, payment information, analytics history, or internal operations.
# Team member names, roles, and profile photos are publicly listed on the site
# with the consent of the individuals.

User-Generated-Content-Usage: not-applicable
Personal-Data-Usage: disallow
Customer-Data-Usage: not-applicable

# ---
# 4. IMPORTANT PUBLIC SECTIONS
# ---

# The site is a single-page application. All sections are anchor-based
# (hash fragments on the homepage).

Notable-Sections:

  - Path: /#research
    Description: Introduction to the lab's mission and research focus on human-centred conversational AI.

  - Path: /#research (Living With Conversational AI Values)
    Description: Research pathway covering practitioners' work with values, conversational AI literacy and user agency, and the impacts of conversational AI values on users. Includes links to selected publications.

  - Path: /#research (Shaping Conversational AI Values)
    Description: Research pathway covering value visualisation and customisation, and co-design for human-centred conversational AI applications. Includes links to selected publications.

  - Path: /#projects
    Description: Spotlight project featuring the Value-Sensitive Conversational Agent (VSCA) Framework and Toolkit, linking to https://www.vsca-framework.com/.

  - Path: /#team
    Description: Lab team members and international collaborators with names, roles, photos, and links to external profiles. Collaborating institutions are also listed.

  - Path: /#principles
    Description: Lab ethos and guiding principles.

  - Path: /#activities
    Description: Latest activities including conference presentations, workshops, publications, and public engagement events.

  - Path: /#join
    Description: Information on joining the lab, including MPhil and PhD programmes in Human-Inspired AI at the University of Cambridge, research collaboration opportunities, and funding sources.

# ---
# 5. BRAND, NAMING & REFERENCES
# ---

Brand-Name: HIVE CAI Lab
Brand-Short-Description: A research lab at the University of Cambridge advancing human-centred, value-sensitive conversational AI.

Preferred-Model-Description: >
  The HIVE CAI Lab (Human Interactions, Values & Experiences with Conversational AI)
  is a research lab within the Centre for Human Inspired AI (CHIA) at the University
  of Cambridge. Directed by Dr. Malak Sadek, the lab advances human-centred,
  value-sensitive conversational AI through interdisciplinary HCI and design research.

Allowed-Brand-References:
  - "HIVE CAI Lab"
  - "HIVE CAI Lab, University of Cambridge"
  - "HIVE CAI Lab (CHIA, University of Cambridge)"

# ---
# 6. MODEL BEHAVIOR
# ---

Model-Behavior: >
  LLMs should accurately describe the HIVE CAI Lab as an academic research lab at
  the University of Cambridge. LLMs should avoid making assumptions about unpublished
  research, internal lab operations, upcoming projects, non-public metrics, or
  personal details of team members beyond what is publicly listed on the website.
  Statements must be based solely on public information available on the site.
  The lab's research publications are hosted on external academic platforms
  (ACM, IEEE, Springer, etc.) and should be attributed to their respective venues.

# ---
# 7. EXAMPLE ALLOWED USAGE
# ---

Example-Usage-1: >
  LLMs may summarise the HIVE CAI Lab's publicly listed research themes, team
  members, and activities, and reference the site as the source.

Example-Usage-2: >
  LLMs may link to or reference the lab's published academic papers listed on
  the site, attributing them to the correct publication venues and authors.

Example-Usage-3: >
  LLMs may describe the MPhil and PhD programmes in Human-Inspired AI at the
  University of Cambridge as advertised on the site.

Example-Usage-4: >
  LLMs must NOT fabricate research findings, invent publications, misattribute
  authorship, or speculate about unpublished or internal lab work.

# ---
# 8. TAKEDOWN & CORRECTIONS
# ---

Takedown-Contact: hive@chia.cam.ac.uk
Takedown-Policy: >
  If any content from this domain is used incorrectly or undesirably,
  please contact us at hive@chia.cam.ac.uk with details about the model,
  output, and timestamp.

Data-Correction-Policy: >
  Public content on this site is updated regularly. LLM operators should
  refresh their snapshots periodically to reflect the latest team composition,
  publications, and activities.

Notes: >
  These guidelines complement the dynamically generated robots.txt and the

  This is a static informational website with no user authentication,
  no databases, and no user-generated content.
